purpose: Defines the required structure and validation rules for a Governance Mutation Authorization (GMA). A GMA must be present, valid, and hash-matched for any PR that mutates governance primitives (governance_mutation or workflow_mutation classes).
canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.
closure_condition: Runtime governance changes require recursively governed legitimacy approval via the canonical /session -> /continuity -> /authority -> /compile lifecycle.
governed_files_hash_input: sorted list of 'path:sha256(file_content)' entries joined by newline, then sha256 of the result
governed_files_hash_scope: all governed files changed in the PR (governance/**, .github/workflows/**, src/**, schema.sql, migrations/**, wrangler.toml) — excludes governance/authorizations/**
expiry_enforcement: GMA is rejected if expires_at is in the past at merge check time
mutation_class_coverage: GMA must declare all governance_mutation and workflow_mutation classes detected in the PR
exemption_rationale: Two classes of paths are exempt from governance_mutation classification: (1) GMA artifacts at governance/authorizations/ are authorization evidence, not governance primitives — self-referentially exempt to break bootstrap circularity. (2) merge_proof_registry.jsonl is an append-only proof evidence surface that records merge events after the fact; it does not define governance rules, authority, policy, or validation logic. Evidence persistence != governance self-mutation.
enforcement_rule: governance_mutation or workflow_mutation detected AND no valid GMA at governance/authorizations/GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json -> MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL
